author_identification.canonical_name_ar: أبو جعفر محمد بن جرير الطبري
author_identification.scholarly_standing: One of the most prominent classical scholars of Islam, renowned for his comprehensive works in tafsir, history, and fiqh.
author_identification.methodological_stance: Known for his meticulous approach to tafsir, combining narrative (riwaya) and analytical (diraya) methods, and for his independence in fiqh rulings.

scholarly_context.tradition_position: A foundational work in tafsir, widely regarded as one of the most comprehensive and authoritative classical commentaries on the Quran.
scholarly_context.historical_significance: Considered a magnum opus in Islamic scholarship, setting a standard for subsequent tafsir works with its extensive use of hadith, linguistic analysis, and comparative fiqh discussions.
scholarly_context.muhaqiq_reputation: Dr. Abdullah bin Abdul Mohsin al-Turki is a highly respected scholar and editor known for his rigorous tahqiq work on major Islamic texts.
scholarly_context.tahqiq_methodology_note: This edition is known for its critical apparatus, including verification of hadith chains, cross-referencing with other classical sources, and detailed footnotes.
